title: Psychotria ankafinensis
taxonomy: Psychotria ankafinensis is a species within the Rubiaceae family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants, with over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species. Psychotria ankafinensis is native to Madagascar and is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree. Its classification as a species within the Rubiaceae family helps to understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Psychotria ankafinensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Ankafinensis or Madagascar rubia. The plant has been traditionally used in Madagascar for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria ankafinensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
